Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

“Image created by ChatGPT”REST APIs is power everything from the mobile apps and e-commerce platforms to banking systems and SaaS platforms. If you’ve started learning Java, building a REST API with Spring Boot is one of the most practical skills you can develop because it’s widely used in modern backend development. Spring Boot removes much of the boilerplate configuration, allowing you to focus on writing business logic instead of setting up infrastructure. In this blog, you’ll build a simple and best REST API step by step while learning the core concepts behind each component. The examples use current Spring Boot practices and are suitable for beginners.
What You’ll Build
A simple Student Management for REST API with the following endpoints:-
MethodEndpointDescriptionGET/studentsGet all studentsGET/students/{id}Get student by IDPOST/studentsCreate a studentPUT/students/{id}Update studentDELETE/students/{id}Delete student
Prerequisites

• Java 17 or later

• IntelliJ IDEA, Eclipse, or VS Code

• Maven

• Basic Java knowledge

• Postman or Insomnia (optional)

• MySQL (optional if using a database)
